pull.title: validation: compact chainstate at minimum chain work
pull.body:
**Problem:** https://github.com/bitcoin-core/leveldb-subtree/pull/61 disabled read-triggered seek compactions to avoid large chainstate write amplification from random UTXO lookups. That avoids repeated read-driven rewrites, but can also leave a less compact LevelDB layout and increase chainstate disk usage after initial sync.

**Fix:** Compact the chainstate once when the connected active chain first reaches the configured minimum chain work. The default minimum chain work is updated with major releases, so this gives initial sync a release-tracking cleanup point without depending on the process-local IBD latch, recurring scheduling, or an assumevalid hash that may be disabled or unavailable. The chainstate is force-flushed before queuing compaction so LevelDB compacts the latest connected state.

**Reproducer:** Setting the minimum work to block height 1000 and stopping at height 5000 should show one compaction start/finish pair:
> build/bin/bitcoind -datadir=$(mktemp -d) -minimumchainwork=3e903e903e9 -stopatheight=5000 -blocksonly 2>&1 | grep -B1 compaction

<details><summary>Expected output</summary>

```text
2026-06-04T17:56:16Z UpdateTip: new best=00000000c937983704a73af28acdec37b049d214adbda81d7e2a3dd146f6ed09 height=1000 version=0x00000001 log2_work=41.967248 tx=1020 date='2009-01-19T06:34:42Z' progress=0.000001 cache=0.4MiB(1015txo)
2026-06-04T17:56:17Z Starting chainstate compaction of /var/folders/5t/04gq0pqj5yv4t8cxw51q0s2m0000gn/T/tmp.ncq50Fdywt/chainstate
--
2026-06-04T17:56:17Z UpdateTip: new best=000000002d5d8c3264d9c6e352b76430cda7a1edac661a515dd1445c15168de5 height=1003 version=0x00000001 log2_work=41.971566 tx=1023 date='2009-01-19T07:26:12Z' progress=0.000001 cache=0.3MiB(3txo)
2026-06-04T17:56:17Z Finished chainstate compaction of /var/folders/5t/04gq0pqj5yv4t8cxw51q0s2m0000gn/T/tmp.ncq50Fdywt/chainstate
```

</details>

coins: test chainstate flush baseline

Add `CDBWrapper::GetProperty()` and expose it through `CCoinsViewDB::GetDBProperty()` so coins tests can inspect LevelDB runtime properties through the coins view.
Use the wrapper helper in `DynamicMemoryUsage()` so existing property access goes through the same path.
Add a coins DB flush baseline that writes one coin and a best block hash, checks they are readable after flushing, and records that `leveldb.num-files-at-level2` is still empty before manual compaction is introduced.

coins: add chainstate compaction method

Add `CDBWrapper::CompactFull()` and `CCoinsViewDB::CompactFull()` so validation code can compact the chainstate without reaching through to the lower-level DB wrapper.
Extend the existing coins DB flush baseline to check that compaction produces a level-2 table file while preserving the flushed coin and best block.

verification.payload:
tree 5a2dc606a8ef18719bed2796bf0d7bc6e838097d
parent d3714bd80eb95d6ca7c6366e0d09a4eb52e53d0c
author Lőrinc <pap.lorinc@gmail.com> 1780593601 +0200
committer Lőrinc <pap.lorinc@gmail.com> 1780597635 +0200

validation: compact chainstate at minimum work

The default minimum chain work is updated with releases and loosely tracks where IBD is expected to end. Without an explicit cleanup point, a node can build most of its chainstate and leave the resulting LevelDB layout to ordinary background compaction.

Queue a full chainstate compaction when the connected active chain first crosses `MinimumChainWork()`, instead of tying the trigger to the process-local IBD latch, recurring scheduling, or an assumevalid hash that may be disabled or unavailable. The chainstate is force-flushed before the callback is queued so the latest state is visible to LevelDB, and the callback skips compaction if shutdown was already requested before it runs.

The functional test configures a low `-minimumchainwork`, checks that no compaction happens before reaching it, expects one compaction start/finish log pair on the crossing block, and verifies that additional blocks do not trigger another compaction.

Co-authored-by: Andrew Toth <andrewstoth@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Antoine Poinsot <darosior@protonmail.com>

comments.body: Could we retrieve the assumed valid block from `m_chainman.GetConsensus().defaultAssumevalid` instead?
That would have made testing more difficult, ended up relying on minimum chainwork instead.

We want the latest in memory chainstate to be consistent with leveldb before we compact, but we don't need to clear the cache. That would needlessly cause a lot more read requests at the same time that we are compacting in the background.

```suggestion
    if (BlockValidationState state; !FlushStateToDisk(state, FlushStateMode::FORCE_SYNC)) { // Ensure compaction reflects the latest chainstate.
```
I'm not sure this change warrants a release note. This change should not be visible to the user.
